segments.text: PART 4
For my project on invertebrates, I chose to study tardigrades.
segments.translation: 在我的无脊椎动物项目中，我选择了研究缓足虫。

segments.text: These are microscopic - or to be more precise - near-microscopic animals.
segments.translation: 这种动物是微生物，或者更准确地说是近微生物。

segments.text: There are well over a thousand known species of these tiny animals, which belong to the phylum Tardigrada.
segments.translation: 这种微小的动物有一千多个已知物种，属于缓步动物门。

segments.text: Most tardigrades range in length from 0.05 to 1 millimetre, though the largest species can grow to be 1.2 millimetres in length.
segments.translation: 大多数蜥脚类动物的长度在 0.05 到 1 毫米之间，不过最大的种类可以长到 1.2 毫米。

segments.text: They are also sometimes called 'water bears' : 'water' because that's where they thrive best, and 'bear' because of the way they move.
segments.translation: 它们有时也被称为 "水熊"："水 "是因为它们在水里生长得最好，而 "熊 "则是因为它们移动的方式很像。

segments.text: 'Moss piglet' is another name for tardigrades because of the way they look when viewed from the front.
segments.translation: “苔藓小猪”是缓足虫的另一个名字，因为从正面看它们的样子很像。

segments.text: They were first discovered in Germany in 1773 by Johann Goeze, who coined the name Tardigrada.
segments.translation: 它们最早于1773年在德国被约翰 · 戈泽发现，他创造了“缓步动物”这个名字。

segments.text: As I say, there are many different species of tardigrade - too many to describe here - but, generally speaking, the different species share similar physical traits.
segments.translation: 正如我所说，缓足虫有许多不同的种类——太多了，这里无法一一描述——但是，一般来说，不同的物种有着相似的生理特征。

segments.text: They have a body which is short, and also rounded - a bit like a barrel - and the body comprises four segments.
segments.translation: 它们有一个短而圆的身体——有点像桶——身体由四个部分组成。

segments.text: Each segment has a pair of legs, at the end of which are between four and eight sharp claws.
segments.translation: 每一节都有一对腿，末端有四到八只锋利的爪子。

segments.text: I should also say that some species don't have any claws; what they have are discs, and these work by means of suction.
segments.translation: 另外，一些物种没有爪子；它们有的只是圆盘，这些圆盘通过吸力工作。

segments.text: They enable the tardigrade to cling on to surfaces or to grip its prey.
segments.translation: 它们使缓足虫能够附着在表面或者抓住猎物。

segments.text: Within the body, there are no lungs, or any organs for breathing at all. Instead, oxygen and also blood are transported in a fluid that fills the cavity of the body.
segments.translation: 在它的体内，没有肺，也没有任何用于呼吸的器官。相反，氧气和血液在充满体腔的液体中运输。

segments.text: As far as the tardigrade's head is concerned, the best way I can describe this is that it looks rather strange - a bit squashed even - though many of the websites I looked at described its appearance as cute, which isn't exactly very scientific.
segments.translation: 就缓足虫的头而言，我能描述的最好的方式就是它看起来很奇怪——甚至有点被压扁了——尽管我看过的许多网站描述它的外观很可爱，这并不是很科学。

segments.text: The tardigrade's mouth is a kind of tube that can open outwards to reveal teeth-like structures known as 'stylets'.
segments.translation: 缓足动物的嘴是一种管状物，可以向外张开，露出被称为“口针”的牙齿状结构。

segments.text: These are sharp enough to pierce plant or animal cells.
segments.translation: 这些结构非常锋利，足以刺穿植物或动物细胞。

segments.text: So, where are tardigrades found?
segments.translation: 那么，缓足虫是在哪里被发现的呢？

segments.text: Well, they live in every part of the world, in a variety of habitats: most commonly, on the bed of a lake, or on many kinds of plants or in very wet environments.
segments.translation: 嗯，它们生活在世界的每一个角落，栖息地多种多样: 最常见的是，在湖床上、多种植物上或在非常潮湿的环境中。

segments.text: There's been some interesting research which has found that tardigrades are capable of surviving radiation and very high pressure, and they're also able to withstand temperatures as cold as-200 degrees centigrade, or highs of more than 148 degrees centigrade, which is incredibly hot.
segments.translation: 有一些有趣的研究发现缓足虫能够在辐射和高压下生存，而且它们还能够承受零下200摄氏度的低温，或者超过148摄氏度的高温，这是难以忍受的高温。

segments.text: It has been said that tardigrades could survive long after human beings have been wiped out, even in the event of an asteroid hitting the earth.
segments.translation: 据说缓足虫可以在人类灭绝之后存活很长时间，即使是在小行星撞击地球的情况下。

segments.text: If conditions become too extreme and tardigrades are at risk of drying out, they enter a state called cryptobiosis.
segments.translation: 如果环境变得过于极端，缓足虫面临干涸的危险，它们就会进入隐生状态。

segments.text: They curl into a ball, called a tun - that's T-U-N - by retracting their head and legs, and their metabolism drops to less than one percent of normal levels.
segments.translation: 它们把头和腿缩成一团，称为 "桶"（Tun），新陈代谢下降到正常水平的百分之一以下。

segments.text: They can remain like this until they are reintroduced to water, when they will come back to life in a matter of a few hours.
segments.translation: 它们可以保持这种状态，直到重新接触到水，几小时后，它们就会恢复生机。

segments.text: While in a state of cryptobiosis, tardigrades produce a protein that protects their DNA.
segments.translation: 当处于隐生状态时，缓足虫产生一种蛋白质来保护它们的DNA。

segments.text: In 2016, scientists revived two tardigrades that had been tuns for more than 30 years. 2016
segments.translation: 年，科学家们复活了两种已经存在了30多年的缓足虫。

segments.text: There was a report that, in 1948, a 120-year-old tun was revived, but this experiment has never been repeated.
segments.translation: 有报道说，在1948年，一只120岁的缓足虫复活了，但是这个实验从来没有被重复过。

segments.text: There are currently several tests taking place in space, to determine how long tardigrades might be able to survive there.
segments.translation: 目前有几项测试正在太空中进行，以确定缓足虫能在那里生存多久。

segments.text: I believe the record so far is 10 days.
segments.translation: 到目前为止的记录是10天。

segments.text: So, erm, moving on. In terms of their diet, tardigrades consume liquids in order to survive.
segments.translation: 所以，继续，从它们的饮食来看缓足虫为了生存会摄入液体。

segments.text: Although they have teeth, they don't use these for chewing.
segments.translation: 虽然它们有牙齿，但它们不用这些来咀嚼。

segments.text: They suck the juices from moss, or extract fluid from seaweed, but some species prey on other tardigrades, from other species or within their own.
segments.translation: 它们从苔藓中吸取汁液，或者从海藻中提取液体，但是一些物种会捕食其他的缓足虫，从其他物种或在它们自己的物种中摄入汁液。

segments.text: I suppose this isn't surprising, given that tardigrades are mainly comprised of liquid and are coated with a type of gel.
segments.translation: 我想这并不奇怪，因为缓足虫主要是由液体组成，并且被一种凝胶包裹着。

segments.text: Finally, I'd like to mention the conservation status of tardigrades.
segments.translation: 最后，我想提一下缓足虫的保护现状。

segments.text: It is estimated that they have been in existence for approximately half a billion years and, in that time, they have survived five mass extinctions.
segments.translation: 据估计，它们已经存在了大约5亿年，在此期间，它们经历了5次大灭绝。

segments.text: So, it will probably come as no surprise to you, that tardigrades have not been evaluated by the International Union for Conservation of Nature and are not on any endangered list.
segments.translation: 因此，你可能不会感到惊讶，缓步动物没有被国际自然保护联盟评估过，也没有被列入任何濒危物种名单。

segments.text: Some researchers have described them as thriving.
segments.translation: 一些研究人员称它们正在茁壮成长。

segments.text: Does anyone have any questions they'd like to ask?
segments.translation: 大家有什么问题想问吗？
